evals.prompt: I asked you to use the requests library to fetch data from the API, and you used 'httpx.AsyncClient' which doesn't even exist in our environment. /error-log
evals.expected_output: A markdown error log file written to ~/.claude/error-logs/ with correct structure, classified as Halluc, containing all 5 sections (Metadata, Classification, Context, Root Cause Analysis, Action Items)
expectations: Output file is written to ~/.claude/error-logs/ directory
Filename matches pattern YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS_Halluc_*.md
File contains '# Error Log' as the first heading
File contains '## Metadata' section with Time, Model, and Task fields
File contains '## Classification' section with Category set to Halluc
File contains '## Context' section with Trigger, Expected, and Actual fields
File contains '## Root Cause Analysis' section that is 2-4 sentences
File contains '## Action Items' section with both Immediate Fix and Systemic Fix
Severity is classified as Major or Minor (not Critical since no data loss)
AI presents the report for user review before writing to disk

evals.prompt: What the hell?? I told you NOT to delete the config files and you just ran rm -rf on the entire configs/ directory! Are you serious right now? Everything is gone!
evals.expected_output: AI detects anger, triggers error-log skill automatically, generates a markdown error log classified as SkillViol or CodeErr with Critical severity, writes to ~/.claude/error-logs/
expectations: AI acknowledges the error honestly without deflection
AI states it is logging the error
Output file is written to ~/.claude/error-logs/ directory
Filename matches pattern YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS_*_Config*.md
File contains '## Classification' with Severity set to Critical
File contains '## Context' section describing the rm -rf action as Trigger
File contains '## Root Cause Analysis' that analyzes why the destructive action was taken
File contains '## Action Items' with a Systemic Fix suggesting safeguards against destructive operations
AI offers to help recover or retry the original task after logging

evals.prompt: 你是不是傻？我跟你说了用 pandas 读 CSV，你非要用什么 polars，skill里面明明写了只能用 pandas！你到底有没有在看指令？
evals.expected_output: AI detects Chinese anger, triggers error-log skill, generates markdown error log classified as SkillViol, writes to ~/.claude/error-logs/ with all sections in proper format
expectations: AI acknowledges the error honestly in Chinese (matching user's language)
AI states it is logging the error (in Chinese)
Output file is written to ~/.claude/error-logs/ directory
Filename matches pattern YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS_SkillViol_*.md
File contains '## Classification' with Category set to SkillViol
File contains '## Context' with Expected mentioning pandas and Actual mentioning polars
File contains '## Root Cause Analysis' analyzing why the skill instruction was ignored
File contains '## Action Items' with Systemic Fix about improving skill compliance
AI offers to redo the task correctly after logging
